meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Mastering User Onboarding (Advanced Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with project ideas. Master high-conversion user onboarding using Antigravity and Claude Code in this advanced developer guide for students.
content.answer_block: Overcome the 'no project ideas' paralysis by building a high-fidelity user onboarding flow. By treating onboarding as a product feature rather than an afterthought, you create a portfolio piece that demonstrates architectural depth, UX empathy, and advanced state management—the exact skills senior engineering leads look for in junior hires.
content.intro:
The blank editor is a graveyard for ambition. You have the technical stack, but you lack the North Star—the project that proves you aren't just a code-monkey, but a product engineer. Most students build 'To-Do' apps; you need to build a system that solves the hardest problem in SaaS: user retention.

User onboarding is the crucible of product design. It forces you to master authentication flows, asynchronous state transitions, and psychological design. When you build a frictionless path from 'Sign Up' to 'Aha! Moment,' you aren't just writing code—you are architecting the bridge between user intent and business value. Let's build it.

steps.title: Define the 'Aha!' Moment Architecture
steps.description: Identify the core value proposition that a user must experience within 60 seconds of login. Map the state transitions required to move a user from unauthenticated to 'activated'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the user journey for a [SaaS Niche] app. Identify the friction points in the initial 60 seconds and propose a 3-step onboarding state machine that minimizes cognitive load.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Create a state machine diagram for onboarding sequence using Mermaid.js syntax.'

steps.title: Build the Progressive Profiling Engine
steps.description: Implement a form-capture system that asks for data only when necessary, preventing drop-offs caused by long sign-up forms.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a React component for a multi-step form that uses progressive profiling. Ensure state persistence across steps using local storage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write --file src/components/OnboardingForm.tsx --logic 'Implement progressive profiling with validation logic.'

steps.title: Implement Context-Aware Tooltips
steps.description: Create a guided tour that triggers based on user interaction rather than forced modals, ensuring the UI feels intuitive.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a custom hook in TypeScript that detects if a user has interacted with the dashboard; if not, trigger a contextual tooltip sequence.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --context 'Use Framer Motion for smooth tooltip transitions and hover-based triggering.'

steps.title: Integrate Skeleton Loading States
steps.description: Improve perceived performance during the onboarding transition to keep users engaged while background tasks complete.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a set of high-performance skeleton loading screens for the onboarding dashboard using Tailwind CSS.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/components/DashboardSkeleton.tsx --style 'Apply pulse animation and gray-200 to gray-400 gradients.'

steps.title: Instrument Telemetry for Drop-off Analysis
steps.description: Add event tracking to your onboarding flow to identify exactly where users abandon the process, turning your project into a data-driven case study.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an analytics wrapper function that logs every step completion in the onboarding flow to a mock database.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Integrate a tracking utility to monitor funnel conversion rates in the onboarding flow.'

pro_tips: Always build your onboarding with a 'Skip' button; forcing users into a guided tour is the fastest way to increase churn.
Use 'optimistic UI' updates for onboarding; the user should feel like the app is ready before the database actually finishes the write operation.
Focus on the 'Empty State'—the dashboard should teach the user how to use the app when they have no data yet, not just show a blank screen.
Implement 'Feature Flags' for your onboarding steps so you can A/B test different flows without redeploying your entire application.
Document your design decisions for the onboarding flow in your README; hiring managers care more about why you chose a specific flow than the code itself.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, deploy your project to Vercel or Netlify and share the link in our community showcase channel for peer code review.
content.conversion_block: You now have a sophisticated, portfolio-ready feature that demonstrates high-level engineering thinking. Don't let this knowledge sit idle—ship it, iterate on it, and show the world you can build products that stick. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
